title: Rinorea cinerea
intro: Rinorea cinerea, also known as the Grey Vinebine, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Violaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its reported analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea cinerea include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rinorea cinerea is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.

This plant belongs to the genus Rinorea, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as violet family plants. Rinorea cinerea is further classified into the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants.
